items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Blickling Hall
*Country:* england
*Region:* east of england
*County:* Norfolk
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 17th century
*Coords:* 52.8118, 1.2318
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blickling_Hall
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Blickling%20Hall/@52.815,1.2233,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/Blickling_Hall_-_south-west_facade.jpg/330px-Blickling_Hall_-_south-west_facade.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* unknown
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/norfolk/blickling-estate

*Description:*
Blickling Hall is a Jacobean stately home situated in 5,000 acres (2,000 ha) of parkland in a loop of the River Bure, near the village of Blickling north of Aylsham in Norfolk, England. The mansion was built on the ruins of a Tudor building for Sir Henry Hobart from 1616 and designed by Robert Lyminge. The library at Blickling Hall contains one of the most historically significant collections of manuscripts and books in England, containing an estimated 13,000 to 14,000 volumes. The core collection was formed by Sir Richard Ellys. The property passed into the care of the National Trust in 1940.

_Listing 1 of 4_

listing.desc: Blickling Hall is a Jacobean stately home situated in 5,000 acres (2,000 ha) of parkland in a loop of the River Bure, near the village of Blickling north of Aylsham in Norfolk, England. The library at Blickling Hall contains one of the most historically significant collections of manuscripts and books in England, containing an estimated 13,000 to 14,000 volumes. The core collection was formed by Sir Richard Ellys. The property passed into the care of the National Trust in 1940.

🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* St Michael's Mount
*Country:* england
*Region:* south west
*County:* Cornwall
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 12th century
*Coords:* 50.116, -5.4772
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Michael%27s_Mount
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/St%20Michael's%20Mount/@50.1175,-5.4776,15z
*Main Image:* none
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* unknown
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://stmichaelsmount.co.uk/

*Description:*
St Michael's Mount is a tidal island in Mount's Bay near Penzance, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. The island is a civil parish and is linked to the town of Marazion by a causeway of granite setts, passable between mid-tide and low water. It is managed by the National Trust, and the castle and chapel have been the home of the St Aubyn family since around 1650.

_Listing 2 of 4_

*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZMrPGTFb4be9XB_84Y1BRr0lm7_swvjl5Kw_nGZE6avMngCtldHZINi6ugwDcXthqA2UfVDmwKhf2x7aVeGYbbD18oXyOG4a2CosSc_JeYCWOf8nTR_zdUmNkatr9Qa2u-oMgGlNCPvMnVDnn8=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/wiltshire/avebury

*Description:*
Avebury Manor & Garden is a National Trust property consisting of a Grade I listed early-16th-century manor house and its surrounding garden. It is in Avebury, near Marlborough, Wiltshire, England, in the centre of the village next to St James's Church and close to the Avebury neolithic henge monument.

_Listing 3 of 4_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Baconsthorpe Castle
*Country:* england
*Region:* east of england
*County:* Norfolk
*Type:* fortified house
*Period:* 15th century
*Coords:* 52.8985, 1.152
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baconsthorpe_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Baconsthorpe%20Castle/@52.8871,1.1514,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZMpx9Jf8rxExeUeBtfBxFc14fPh2JLuRN8iDPbKENhYpe9M7ez1NeXShmQ2ufangTEt5mYaYhD1umJbc-3vqcRxUtFoP23oQpdG1VeLp6edPHT1foBtsvO_p7a1aZOCIV2p8X-NxRBvl8QdfQ=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/baconsthorpe-castle/

*Description:*
Baconsthorpe Castle, historically known as Baconsthorpe Hall, is a ruined, fortified manor house near the village of Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England. It was established in the 15th century on the site of a former manor hall, probably by John Heydon I and his father, William. John was an ambitious lawyer with many enemies and built a tall, fortified house, but his descendants became wealthy sheep farmers, and being less worried about attack, developed the property into a more elegant, courtyard house, complete with a nearby deer park.

_Listing 4 of 4_

John was an ambitious lawyer with many enemies and built a tall, fortified house, but his descendants became wealthy sheep farmers, and being less worried about attack, developed the property into a more elegant, courtyard house, complete with a nearby deer park.
